heading: Manufacturer discount program
label: 1395w–114c Manufacturer discount program
notes: References in TextSection 52 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, referred to in subsec. (g)(4)(B)(ii)(II)(bb), (C)(ii)(II)(bb), is classified to , Internal Revenue Code.section 52 of Title 26

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a manufacturer discount program (in this section referred to as the “program”). Under the program, the Secretary shall enter into agreements described in subsection (b) with manufacturers and provide for the performance of the duties described in subsection (c).

children.heading: Agreement
children.label: A Agreement
children.text: An agreement under this section shall require the manufacturer to provide, in accordance with this section, discounted prices for applicable drugs of the manufacturer that are dispensed to applicable beneficiaries on or after .January 1, 2025

children.label: i
children.text: the application of a coinsurance of 25 percent of the negotiated price, as applied under paragraph (2)(A) of , for costs described in such paragraph; orsection 1395w–102(b) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the application of the copayment amount described in paragraph (4)(A) of such section, with respect to costs described in such paragraph.

children.heading: Special rule for 2025
children.label: i Special rule for 2025
children.text: In order for an agreement with a manufacturer to be in effect under this section with respect to the period beginning on , and ending on , the manufacturer shall enter into such agreement not later than .January 1, 2025December 31, 2025March 1, 2024

children.heading: 2026 and subsequent years
children.label: ii 2026 and subsequent years
children.text: In order for an agreement with a manufacturer to be in effect under this section with respect to plan year 2026 or a subsequent plan year, the manufacturer shall enter into such agreement not later than a calendar quarter or semi-annual deadline established by the Secretary.

children.heading: Provision of appropriate data
children.label: 2 Provision of appropriate data
children.text: Each manufacturer with an agreement in effect under this section shall collect and have available appropriate data, as determined by the Secretary, to ensure that it can demonstrate to the Secretary compliance with the requirements under the program.

children.heading: Compliance with requirements for administration of program
children.label: 3 Compliance with requirements for administration of program
children.text: Each manufacturer with an agreement in effect under this section shall comply with requirements imposed by the Secretary, as applicable, for purposes of administering the program, including any determination under subparagraph (A) of subsection (c)(1) or procedures established under such subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An agreement under this section shall be effective for an initial period of not less than 12 months and shall be automatically renewed for a period of not less than 1 year unless terminated under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: By the Secretary
children.label: i By the Secretary
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for termination of an agreement under this section for a knowing and willful violation of the requirements of the agreement or other good cause shown. Such termination shall not be effective earlier than 30 days after the date of notice to the manufacturer of such termination. The Secretary shall provide, upon request, a manufacturer with a hearing concerning such a termination, and such hearing shall take place prior to the effective date of the termination with sufficient time for such effective date to be repealed if the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: I
children.text: if the termination occurs before January 31 of a plan year, as of the day after the end of the plan year; and

children.label: II
children.text: if the termination occurs on or after January 31 of a plan year, as of the day after the end of the succeeding plan year.

children.heading: Effectiveness of termination
children.label: iii Effectiveness of termination
children.text: Any termination under this subparagraph shall not affect discounts for applicable drugs of the manufacturer that are due under the agreement before the effective date of its termination.

children.heading: Effective date of agreement
children.label: 5 Effective date of agreement
children.text: An agreement under this section shall take effect at the start of a calendar quarter or another date specified by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: the determination of the amount of the discounted price of an applicable drug of a manufacturer;

children.label: i
children.text: the negotiated price of the applicable drug; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the discounted price of the applicable drug;

children.label: C
children.text: the establishment of procedures to ensure that the discounted price for an applicable drug under this section is applied before any coverage or financial assistance under other health benefit plans or programs that provide coverage or financial assistance for the purchase or provision of prescription drug coverage on behalf of applicable beneficiaries as specified by the Secretary; and

children.label: D
children.text: providing a reasonable dispute resolution mechanism to resolve disagreements between manufacturers, prescription drug plans and MA–PD plans, and the Secretary.

children.heading: Monitoring compliance
children.label: 2 Monitoring compliance
children.text: The Secretary shall monitor compliance by a manufacturer with the terms of an agreement under this section.

children.heading: Collection of data from prescription drug plans and MA–PD plans
children.label: 3 Collection of data from prescription drug plans and MA–PD plans
children.text: The Secretary may collect appropriate data from prescription drug plans and MA–PD plans in a timeframe that allows for discounted prices to be provided for applicable drugs under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the Secretary shall provide for the implementation of this section, including the performance of the duties described in subsection (c).

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: In providing for the implementation of this section, the Secretary shall not receive or distribute any funds of a manufacturer under the program.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount that the manufacturer would have paid with respect to such discounts under the agreement, which will then be used to pay the discounts which the manufacturer had failed to provide; and

children.label: B
children.text: 25 percent of such amount.

children.heading: Application
children.label: 2 Application
children.text: The provisions of (other than subsections (a) and (b)) shall apply to a civil money penalty under this subsection in the same manner as such provisions apply to a penalty or proceeding under .section 1320a–7a of this titlesection 1320a–7a(a) of this title

children.heading: Clarification regarding availability of other covered part D drugs
children.label: f Clarification regarding availability of other covered part D drugs
children.text: Nothing in this section shall prevent an applicable beneficiary from purchasing a covered part D drug that is not an applicable drug (including a generic drug or a drug that is not on the formulary of the prescription drug plan or MA–PD plan that the applicable beneficiary is enrolled in).

children.label: A
children.text: is enrolled in a prescription drug plan or an MA–PD plan;

children.label: B
children.text: is not enrolled in a qualified retiree prescription drug plan; and

children.label: C
children.text: has incurred costs, as determined in accordance with , for covered part D drugs in the year that exceed the annual deductible specified in .section 1395w–102(b)(4)(C) of this titlesection 1395w–102(b)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: approved under a new drug application under or, in the case of a biologic product, licensed under ; andsection 355(c) of title 21section 262 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: if the PDP sponsor of the prescription drug plan or the MA organization offering the MA–PD plan uses a formulary, which is on the formulary of the prescription drug plan or MA–PD plan that the applicable beneficiary is enrolled in;

children.label: II
children.text: if the PDP sponsor of the prescription drug plan or the MA organization offering the MA–PD plan does not use a formulary, for which benefits are available under the prescription drug plan or MA–PD plan that the applicable beneficiary is enrolled in; or

children.label: III
children.text: is provided through an exception or appeal; and

children.label: B
children.text: does not include a selected drug (as referred to under ) during a price applicability period (as defined in ) with respect to such drug.section 1320f–1(c) of this titlesection 1320f(b)(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to claims for reimbursement submitted electronically, 14 days; and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to claims for reimbursement submitted otherwise, 30 days.

children.label: i
children.text: who has not incurred costs, as determined in accordance with , for covered part D drugs in the year that are equal to or exceed the annual out-of-pocket threshold specified in for the year, 90 percent of the negotiated price of such drug; andsection 1395w–102(b)(4)(C) of this titlesection 1395w–102(b)(4)(B)(i) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: who has incurred such costs, as so determined, in the year that are equal to or exceed such threshold for the year, 80 percent of the negotiated price of such drug.

children.heading: Phase-in for certain drugs dispensed to LIS beneficiaries
children.label: B Phase-in for certain drugs dispensed to LIS beneficiaries

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of an applicable drug of a specified manufacturer (as defined in clause (ii)) that is marketed as of , and dispensed for an applicable beneficiary who is a subsidy eligible individual (as defined in ), the term “discounted price” means the specified LIS percent (as defined in clause (iii)) of the negotiated price of the applicable drug of the manufacturer.August 16, 2022section 1395w–114(a)(3) of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: the manufacturer had a coverage gap discount agreement under ;section 1395w–114a of this title

children.label: bb
children.text: the total expenditures for all of the specified drugs of the manufacturer covered by such agreement or agreements for such year and covered under this part during such year represented less than 1.0 percent of the total expenditures under this part for all covered Part D drugs during such year; and1So in original. Probably should not be capitalized.1

children.label: cc
children.text: the total expenditures for all of the specified drugs of the manufacturer that are single source drugs and biological products for which payment may be made under part B during such year represented less than 1.0 percent of the total expenditures under part B for all drugs or biological products for which payment may be made under such part during such year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: aa In general
children.text: For purposes of this clause, the term “specified drug” means, with respect to a specified manufacturer, for 2021, an applicable drug that is produced, prepared, propagated, compounded, converted, or processed by the manufacturer.

children.heading: Aggregation rule
children.label: bb Aggregation rule
children.text: All persons treated as a single employer under subsection (a) or (b) of section 52 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall be treated as one manufacturer for purposes of this subparagraph. For purposes of making a determination pursuant to the previous sentence, an agreement under this section shall require that a manufacturer provide and attest to such information as specified by the Secretary as necessary.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: III Limitation
children.text: The term “specified manufacturer” shall not include a manufacturer described in subclause (I) if such manufacturer is acquired after 2021 by another manufacturer that is not a specified manufacturer, effective at the beginning of the plan year immediately following such acquisition or, in the case of an acquisition before 2025, effective .January 1, 2025

children.label: ee

children.heading: Phase-in for specified small manufacturers
children.label: C Phase-in for specified small manufacturers

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of an applicable drug of a specified small manufacturer (as defined in clause (ii)) that is marketed as of , and dispensed for an applicable beneficiary, the term “discounted price” means the specified small manufacturer percent (as defined in clause (iii)) of the negotiated price of the applicable drug of the manufacturer.August 16, 2022

children.label: aa
children.text: the manufacturer is a specified manufacturer (as defined in subparagraph (B)(ii)); and

children.label: bb
children.text: the total expenditures under part D for any one of the specified small manufacturer drugs of the manufacturer that are covered by the agreement or agreements under of such manufacturer for such year and covered under this part during such year are equal to or more than 80 percent of the total expenditures under this part for all specified small manufacturer drugs of the manufacturer that are covered by such agreement or agreements for such year and covered under this part during such year.section 1395w–114a of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: aa In general
children.text: For purposes of this clause, the term “specified small manufacturer drugs” means, with respect to a specified small manufacturer, for 2021, an applicable drug that is produced, prepared, propagated, compounded, converted, or processed by the manufacturer.

children.heading: Aggregation rule
children.label: bb Aggregation rule
children.text: All persons treated as a single employer under subsection (a) or (b) of section 52 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall be treated as one manufacturer for purposes of this subparagraph. For purposes of making a determination pursuant to the previous sentence, an agreement under this section shall require that a manufacturer provide and attest to such information as specified by the Secretary as necessary.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: III Limitation
children.text: The term “specified small manufacturer” shall not include a manufacturer described in subclause (I) if such manufacturer is acquired after 2021 by another manufacturer that is not a specified small manufacturer, effective at the beginning of the plan year immediately following such acquisition or, in the case of an acquisition before 2025, effective .January 1, 2025

children.heading: Total expenditures
children.label: D Total expenditures
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “total expenditures” includes, in the case of expenditures with respect to part D, the total gross covered prescription drug costs as defined in . The term “total expenditures” excludes, in the case of expenditures with respect to part B, expenditures for a drug or biological that are bundled or packaged into the payment for another service.section 1395w–115(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: Special case for certain claims
children.label: E Special case for certain claims

children.heading: Claims spanning deductible
children.label: i Claims spanning deductible
children.text: In the case where the entire amount of the negotiated price of an individual claim for an applicable drug with respect to an applicable beneficiary does not fall above the annual deductible specified in for the year, the manufacturer of the applicable drug shall provide the discounted price under this section on only the portion of the negotiated price of the applicable drug that falls above such annual deductible.section 1395w–102(b)(1) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: in accordance with subparagraph (A)(i) on the portion of the negotiated price of the applicable drug that falls below such threshold; and

children.label: II
children.text: in accordance with subparagraph (A)(ii) on the portion of such price of such drug that falls at or above such threshold.

children.heading: Manufacturer
children.label: 5 Manufacturer
children.text: The term “manufacturer” means any entity which is engaged in the production, preparation, propagation, compounding, conversion, or processing of prescription drug products, either directly or indirectly by extraction from substances of natural origin, or independently by means of chemical synthesis, or by a combination of extraction and chemical synthesis. Such term does not include a wholesale distributor of drugs or a retail pharmacy licensed under State law.

children.heading: Negotiated price
children.label: 6 Negotiated price
children.text: The term “negotiated price” has the meaning given such term for purposes of , and, with respect to an applicable drug, such negotiated price shall include any dispensing fee and, if applicable, any vaccine administration fee for the applicable drug.section 1395w–102(d)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Qualified retiree prescription drug plan
children.label: 7 Qualified retiree prescription drug plan
children.text: The term “qualified retiree prescription drug plan” has the meaning given such term in .section 1395w–132(a)(2) of this title
